arguments.task: Visit official SendGrid and Twilio SendGrid documentation pages to verify API authentication methods, API reference surface, pricing/access availability, rate limits, and whether there is any official MCP/server mention. Collect exact supporting URLs from first-party pages only. Also check official GitHub organization for any MCP/server repositories, and search public MCP registry or GitHub only if needed after first-party pages.

raw_result.description: Cloud email delivery platform with APIs for transactional email, contacts, sender management, and marketing workflows.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official v3 API authentication docs indicate requests use an Authorization header with a Bearer token backed by a SendGrid API key.
raw_result.gating_reason: Official pricing page shows a self-serve Free Trial plan and standard paid tiers (Essentials, Pro) without contact-sales gating for basic API access.

api_surface.breadth: Public SendGrid v3 API reference with multiple resource groups visible in the official API reference, including mail send, marketing/contacts, templates, suppression management, sender identities, stats, and security-related endpoints. Evidence gathered supports broad REST coverage, but exact full inventory was not exhaustively verified in this run.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official MCP or Model Context Protocol server was found in the gathered first-party docs evidence during this run. This remains a negative-by-absence finding and should be human-checked if MCP status is critical.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Official rate-limit docs indicate SendGrid returns header-based limits per request, including X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining, and X-RateLimit-Reset. Specific numeric ceilings were not conclusively captured from the gathered evidence, suggesting endpoint-dependent limits and warranting human review for exact operational thresholds.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official docs expose a dedicated API reference and separate pages for authentication and rate limits; pricing is self-serve with free-trial and paid tiers; official GitHub organizations exist for SendGrid/Twilio SDK and ecosystem presence.
raw_result.human_review_reason: MCP existence was inferred only from absence in gathered evidence rather than a definitive official statement, and exact rate-limit numbers plus complete API breadth were not exhaustively verified from the already collected pages.

result.description: Cloud email delivery platform with APIs for transactional email, contacts, sender management, and marketing workflows.
result.auth_notes: Official v3 API authentication docs indicate requests use an Authorization header with a Bearer token backed by a SendGrid API key.
result.gating_reason: Official pricing page shows a self-serve Free Trial plan and standard paid tiers (Essentials, Pro) without contact-sales gating for basic API access.

api_surface.breadth: Public SendGrid v3 API reference with multiple resource groups visible in the official API reference, including mail send, marketing/contacts, templates, suppression management, sender identities, stats, and security-related endpoints. Evidence gathered supports broad REST coverage, but exact full inventory was not exhaustively verified in this run.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official MCP or Model Context Protocol server was found in the gathered first-party docs evidence during this run. This remains a negative-by-absence finding and should be human-checked if MCP status is critical.

result.rate_limit_notes: Official rate-limit docs indicate SendGrid returns header-based limits per request, including X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining, and X-RateLimit-Reset. Specific numeric ceilings were not conclusively captured from the gathered evidence, suggesting endpoint-dependent limits and warranting human review for exact operational thresholds.
result.notable_signals: Official docs expose a dedicated API reference and separate pages for authentication and rate limits; pricing is self-serve with free-trial and paid tiers; official GitHub organizations exist for SendGrid/Twilio SDK and ecosystem presence.
result.human_review_reason: MCP existence was inferred only from absence in gathered evidence rather than a definitive official statement, and exact rate-limit numbers plus complete API breadth were not exhaustively verified from the already collected pages.
